name: Southern Nations, Nationalities and Peoples' Region (Ethiopia)
description: Three horizontal bands of blue, white, and red, with a brown traditional thatched-roof hut (tukul) in the centre.
history: The Southern Nations, Nationalities, and Peoples' Region (SNNPR) was one of the regional states of Ethiopia, established under the 1995 constitution and home to dozens of ethnic groups in the south-west, with its capital at Hawassa. It was progressively dissolved between 2019 and 2023 as its zones voted to form the Sidama, South West Ethiopia, South Ethiopia, and Central Ethiopia regions.
